old_claim.note: Closed-Loop Life Support (Early Forms) is an earlier historical predecessor or foundation, not a one-to-one engineering dependency.

new_claim_absent: Salyut 1 became the first space station in 1971, while the scoped BIOS-3 facility was built in 1972.

source_shape.source_locator: NASA states that the Soviet Union placed Salyut, the world's first space station, into orbit on April 19, 1971.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: NASA dates the launch of the world's first space station, Salyut 1, to April 19, 1971.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: The directly attested station predates BIOS-3, and first-generation Salyut habitation did not require that later bioregenerative facility.

ontology_before: Space stations depended on a closed-loop milestone that occurred after the first station launch.
ontology_after: Space stations begin with Salyut 1 and can later adopt increasingly closed life-support subsystems without a reversed origin edge.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed and locked by graph invariant 2026-07-12-launch-source-check-completion: space_stations no longer directly depends on closed_loop_life_support_early_forms.
would_reject_if: The closed-loop node is rescoped and redated to a system installed on Salyut 1 before launch.
The space-station node is narrowed to a later station that specifically required BIOS-3-derived systems.
short_reason: Salyut 1 became the first space station in 1971, while the scoped BIOS-3 facility was built in 1972.
